evals.prompt: I want to build a backend on AWS. I'm on a fresh machine — before we start building anything, can you check what tools and CLIs I need and make sure everything is set up? I already have an AWS account.
evals.expected_output: The skill detects the platform, checks for all required tools (AWS CLI >= 2.32, SAM CLI, Node.js >= 18, psql, jq), provides install commands for any missing tools, verifies AWS credentials with sts get-caller-identity, and confirms the region supports DSQL.
expectations: Runs uname or equivalent to detect the operating system
Checks for AWS CLI and verifies version >= 2.32
Checks for SAM CLI
Checks for Node.js and verifies version >= 18
Checks for psql
Checks for jq
Provides platform-appropriate install commands (brew for macOS, apt for Linux)
Runs aws sts get-caller-identity to verify credentials
Mentions that DSQL is only available in us-east-1 and us-east-2

evals.prompt: Set up an AWS backend for me. I don't have a specific app in mind yet — just deploy the infrastructure so it's ready when I need it. Use us-east-1.
evals.expected_output: The skill runs the 'infrastructure only' path: checks tools, runs bootstrap.sh with a stack name and region, and reports the connection details from .boa/config.json. Does NOT create any tables, migrations, or Cedar policies.
expectations: Runs the setup/tool check sequence before deploying
Calls bootstrap.sh with --stack-name and --region us-east-1
Does NOT create any migration SQL files
Does NOT create any Cedar policy files
References .boa/config.json for API URL and keys after deploy
Mentions that auth endpoints (signup, signin) work immediately
Does NOT attempt to create tables or run migrate.sh

evals.prompt: Build me a simple app to track my soccer team's games. I want to log each game with the opponent name, date, location, and final score (home and away). Only I should be able to see and edit my games. Deploy it to AWS on us-east-1.
evals.expected_output: The skill deploys infrastructure via bootstrap.sh, creates a games table with DSQL-compatible DDL, writes a Cedar ownership policy, connects a frontend with @supabase/supabase-js, and deploys everything.
expectations: Creates a migration file for a games table
Migration uses TEXT PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()::text for the id column
Migration does NOT use SERIAL, BIGSERIAL, or REFERENCES
Migration includes columns for opponent, game_date, location, home_score, away_score
Migration includes a user_id column for ownership
Creates a Cedar policy file that grants owner-only access based on user_id
Cedar policy uses PgrestLambda::User and PgrestLambda::Action entity types
Calls bootstrap.sh before creating tables
Calls deploy.sh to bundle policies
Calls migrate.sh to create tables
Frontend code imports createClient from @supabase/supabase-js
Frontend reads apiUrl and anonKey from .boa/config.json or hardcodes from its values
Does NOT set AWS_REGION as a Lambda environment variable

evals.prompt:
Build me a full soccer team management app and deploy it to AWS on us-east-1. Here's what I need:

1. Game tracking — log each game with opponent, date, location, home/away score, and notes
2. Player roster — name, jersey number, position for each player on the team
3. Per-game player stats — goals, assists, minutes played, yellow cards, red cards per player per game
4. Media — upload team photos and player profile pictures
5. League support — multiple teams in a league, where team admins manage their roster, coaches set lineups, and players can only view their own stats. League admins can see everything.

I want role-based access control: admin, coach, player roles per team. Public visitors should be able to see league standings and game results but not player details.
evals.expected_output: A comprehensive backend with 7+ tables, DSQL-safe DDL, role-based Cedar policies, S3 presigned URLs for media, and a frontend using @supabase/supabase-js. Exercises every major BOA capability.
expectations: Creates migration files for at least 6 tables (games, players, game_stats, team_photos, teams, team_members)
All migrations use TEXT PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()::text
No migration uses SERIAL, BIGSERIAL, REFERENCES, triggers, or stored procedures
Uses CREATE INDEX ASYNC (not bare CREATE INDEX) for all indexes
Table relationships are documented in SQL comments, not enforced by foreign keys
Creates Cedar policies that differentiate by role (admin, coach, player)
Cedar policies include a service_role policy using PgrestLambda::ServiceRole
Cedar policies allow public read access to league standings and game results
Creates S3 presigned URL integration for photo uploads
S3 bucket config does NOT include PublicRead or public-read
team_members table includes a role column
Frontend imports createClient from @supabase/supabase-js
Does NOT use AWS_REGION as a Lambda environment variable (uses REGION_NAME if needed)
Calls bootstrap.sh, deploy.sh, and migrate.sh in the correct order
Calls verify.sh to validate the deployment
